questions.question: Supposing if it rains, what will we do about the outdoor garden party?
questions.explanation: 'Supposing' and 'if' mean the same; use only one. / 'Supposing' और 'if' दोनों का अर्थ समान है, एक साथ प्रयोग न करें।

questions.question: No sooner did the thief see the policeman when he took to his heels.
questions.explanation: 'No sooner' is always followed by 'than'. / 'No sooner' के साथ हमेशा 'than' का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: The teacher asked the students to not make a noise in the corridor.
questions.explanation: The correct infinitive structure is 'not to make'. / सही infinitive संरचना 'not to make' है।

questions.question: Considering the rapid depletion of resources, it is high time that the global community realizes the gravity of the situation.
options: of our natural resources, it is high time
questions.explanation: After 'it is high time', use the past simple form 'realized'. / 'It is high time' के बाद हमेशा past simple (V2) 'realized' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: She is the tallest of the two sisters who have been living in this city for over a decade now.
options: in this city for over a decade
questions.explanation: Use 'the taller' for two persons; 'tallest' is for more than two. / दो के लिए 'the taller' का प्रयोग करें; 'tallest' दो से अधिक के लिए होता है।

questions.question: The more he works, the most he earns, which allows him to support his large family comfortably.
questions.explanation: Use 'the more... the more' for parallel increase. / समानांतर वृद्धि दिखाने के लिए 'the more... the more' का प्रयोग आवश्यक है।

questions.question: Senior citizens prefer coffee than tea, especially during the winter season.
questions.explanation: 'Prefer' is followed by the preposition 'to', not 'than'. / 'Prefer' के साथ हमेशा 'to' का प्रयोग होता है, 'than' का नहीं।

questions.question: The more you explore, the more adventurous you become, as stepping outside comfort zone introduce you to new cultures.
options: The more you explore, the more
questions.explanation: 'Stepping' (gerund) is a singular subject, so use 'introduces'. / 'Stepping' एकवचन subject है, इसलिए 'introduce' की जगह 'introduces' होगा।

questions.question: He is one of the tallest boy in the class who always scores top marks.
questions.explanation: 'One of the' is followed by a plural noun 'boys'. / 'One of the' के बाद हमेशा बहुवचन noun 'boys' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: The climate of Shimla is cooler than Delhi, making it a popular tourist spot.
questions.explanation: Compare climate to climate; use 'than that of Delhi'. / शिमला की जलवायु की तुलना दिल्ली की जलवायु से करें, 'than that of Delhi' लिखें।

questions.question: The furniture in this room are very old and needs to be replaced immediately.
questions.explanation: 'Furniture' is an uncountable noun and always takes a singular verb 'is'. / 'Furniture' अगणनीय है और हमेशा 'is' (singular verb) लेता है।

questions.question: Neither the players nor the coach are happy with the decision made by the referee.
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with the nearest subject 'coach', so use 'is'. / क्रिया अपने नजदीकी subject 'coach' के अनुसार 'is' होनी चाहिए।

questions.question: It is high time that the government adopts sustainable practices to ensure survival.
questions.explanation: 'It is high time' requires V2 (past form), so use 'adopted'. / 'It is high time' के साथ क्रिया का दूसरा रूप 'adopted' प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: She is the taller than any other girl in her class, making her perfect for the basketball team.
questions.explanation: Remove 'the' before 'taller' when followed by 'than'. / 'Than' के साथ तुलना करते समय comparative degree से पहले 'the' नहीं आता।

questions.question: You should avail of this opportunity to improve your communication skills.
questions.explanation: 'Avail' takes a reflexive pronoun; use 'avail yourself of'. / 'Avail' के साथ reflexive pronoun (yourself) का प्रयोग जरूरी है।

questions.question: The news of his sudden demise were broadcasted on all national television channels.
questions.explanation: 'News' is singular, use 'was'. Also, 'broadcast' is the same in all forms. / 'News' एकवचन है, 'was' का प्रयोग करें और 'broadcasted' गलत शब्द है।

questions.question: Ramesh is the best of the two candidates with exceptional skills and strong work ethics that set him apart from his competition.
options: Ramesh is the best of the two candidates
with exceptional skills and strong work ethics
that set him apart from his competition
questions.explanation: Use 'better' instead of 'best' when comparing two people. / दो व्यक्तियों की तुलना के लिए 'best' के स्थान पर 'better' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: I am looking forward to meet you at the upcoming annual conference.
questions.explanation: 'Looking forward to' is followed by a gerund; use 'meeting'. / 'Looking forward to' के बाद gerund 'meeting' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Many a man have lost their lives in the attempt to scale the Everest.
questions.explanation: 'Many a' is followed by a singular noun and singular verb 'has'. / 'Many a' के साथ एकवचन noun और 'has' (singular verb) आता है।

questions.question: Scarcely had he gone out than it began to rain heavily, ruining all his plans for the day.
options: all his plans for the day
questions.explanation: 'Scarcely' is always followed by 'when', not 'than'. / 'Scarcely' के साथ हमेशा 'when' का प्रयोग होता है, 'than' का नहीं।

questions.question: He is enough tall to reach the top shelf without using a ladder.
questions.explanation: 'Enough' comes after the adjective; use 'tall enough'. / 'Enough' हमेशा adjective के बाद आता है; 'tall enough' लिखें।

questions.question: She has a daughter who are an artist and a son who works as a software engineer in London.
options: who works as a software engineer
questions.explanation: 'Daughter' is singular, so the relative pronoun 'who' takes 'is'. / 'Daughter' एकवचन है, इसलिए 'who' के बाद 'is' का प्रयोग होगा।

questions.question: In case of choice between these two roads, we should take the one that is shortest.
questions.explanation: For a choice between two, use 'the shorter'. / दो के बीच चुनाव (choice) दर्शाने के लिए 'the shorter' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: He told to me that he would arrive late due to heavy traffic.
questions.explanation: 'Told' is a transitive verb; remove 'to' after it. / 'Told' के साथ 'to' का प्रयोग नहीं होता है।

questions.question: I have seen him yesterday at the supermarket with his younger brother.
questions.explanation: With past time markers like 'yesterday', use simple past 'saw'. / 'Yesterday' जैसे शब्दों के साथ simple past 'saw' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: If I was the Prime Minister of India, I would work for the welfare of the poor.
questions.explanation: In imaginary sentences, use 'were' with all subjects. / काल्पनिक वाक्यों में सभी subjects के साथ 'were' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Not only he but also his friends was present at the party last night.
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with the second subject 'friends', so use 'were'. / क्रिया दूसरे विषय 'friends' के अनुसार 'were' होनी चाहिए।

questions.question: Neither of the two candidates were found suitable for the post of manager.
questions.explanation: 'Neither' refers to 'none of the two' and takes a singular verb 'was'. / 'Neither' एकवचन भाव देता है, इसलिए 'was' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Teaching students are not a child's play, especially when they are not interested in the subject.
questions.explanation: The subject is 'Teaching' (gerund), which is singular, so use 'is'. / यहाँ मुख्य subject 'Teaching' है, जो एकवचन है, इसलिए 'is' आएगा।

questions.question: Unless you do not work hard, you will not be able to clear the competitive examination.
questions.explanation: 'Unless' is negative; do not use 'not' with it. / 'Unless' स्वयं नकारात्मक है, इसके साथ 'not' का प्रयोग वर्जित है।

questions.question: Despite of his hard work, he failed to secure the first rank in the class.
questions.explanation: 'Despite' does not take the preposition 'of'. / 'Despite' के साथ 'of' का प्रयोग नहीं किया जाता है।

questions.question: Knowledge of at least two languages are required to pass this competitive exam.
questions.explanation: 'Knowledge' is the singular subject; use 'is'. / मुख्य subject 'Knowledge' है, जो एकवचन है, अतः 'is' आएगा।

questions.question: Ten miles are a long distance to cover on foot for a small child.
questions.explanation: A specific distance is treated as a single unit, use 'is'. / एक निश्चित दूरी को एक इकाई माना जाता है, इसलिए 'is' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: One should keep his promise if he wants to be respected in society.
questions.explanation: 'One' as a subject takes 'one's' as a possessive. / 'One' के लिए possessive pronoun 'one's' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: My elder brother is more stronger than me when it comes to weightlifting.
questions.explanation: Avoid double comparatives; use only 'stronger'. / Double comparatives से बचें; केवल 'stronger' का प्रयोग पर्याप्त है।

questions.question: Each of the students have contributed to the relief fund for the flood victims.
questions.explanation: 'Each of' always takes a singular verb, so use 'has'. / 'Each of' के बाद हमेशा एकवचन क्रिया 'has' का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: This is the same book which I bought from the bookstore last week.
questions.explanation: After 'the same', use 'that' as the relative pronoun. / 'The same' के बाद 'that' का प्रयोग relative pronoun के रूप में करें।

questions.question: The quality of these mangoes are not as good as the ones we bought yesterday.
questions.explanation: The subject is 'quality' (singular), so the verb must be 'is'. / मुख्य विषय 'quality' (एकवचन) है, इसलिए क्रिया 'is' होगी।

questions.question: Although he was ill, but he went to work to finish the pending project.
questions.explanation: 'Although' is followed by 'yet' or a comma, not 'but'. / 'Although' के साथ 'yet' या कोमा आता है, 'but' नहीं।

questions.question: He has been living in this house since five years with his parents.
questions.explanation: Use 'for' for a duration of time (five years). / समय की अवधि (five years) के लिए 'for' का प्रयोग करें।
